(fl6) m-5662 
m.5660 
m.s901 
Arrece Jameson, City Clerk 
City of Oakland 
1421 Washington Street 
Oakland, CA 
94612 
Dear Arrece: 
September 18, 1985 
•. w.. •••• , 
This is to confirm the information I provided to you last 
week regarding the Statements of Economic Interests (Form 730) 
filed by designated employees pursuant to the City of Oakland's 
Conflict of Interest Code. 
As we discussed, the Form 730 prepared by the City Clerk's 
office is incomplete and should not be used by designated 
employees to fulfill their disclosure obligations under the 
Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015). 
The Conflict of Interest Code and Commission regulations require 
that Statements of Economic Interests shall be made on forms 
prescribed by the Fair Political Practices Commission. 
Therefore, 
designated employees should use only the Form 730 sent to you by 
our office. 
You may, of course, add your own cover memorandum 
of other information you think would be helpful. 
Because the City's Form 730 was used by designated employees 
to file their Initial Statements of Economic Interests, and 
because that form did not contain all of the schedules necessary 
for them to fully and properly disclose their financial interests, 
an amended Initial Statement should be filed by each employee. 
Probably the easiest way to accomplish this would be to provide 
the FPPC Form 730 and request that a new Statement be filed. 
Enclosed is the Commission's Handbook for Filing Officers 
and Filing Officials which explains your duties under the Act 
with respect to Statements of Economic Interests. 
Also enclosed 
are the pages from your list of disclosure categories which I 
marked up for you and inadvertently brought back to Sacramento 
with me. 


Arrece Jameson 
September 18, 1985 
Page 2 
Thanks so much for your hospitality and for everyone's 
help during the campaign disclosure seminar. 
Please let me 
know if I can be of any assistance to you in the future. 
Sincerely, 
(]~ 
Carla Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 
Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the City of Oakland's locally-prepared Form 730 for Statements of Economic Interests is compliant with FPPC requirements and whether designated employees can use it to fulfill their disclosure obligations.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The City of Oakland's Form 730 is incomplete and does not comply with the Political Reform Act. Designated employees must use only the FPPC-prescribed Form 730, and those who previously filed using the city's incomplete form should file amended Initial Statements using the proper FPPC form.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a follow-up confirmation letter rather than a response to a formal written request. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present, but the substance of the advice is clear from the body. Letter dated September 18, 1985, from FPPC Political Reform Consultant Carla Wardlow to City of Oakland City Clerk.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the City of Oakland's locally-prepared Form 730 for Statements of Economic Interests is compliant with FPPC requirements and whether designated employees can use it to fulfill their disclosure obligations.

CONCLUSION: The City of Oakland's Form 730 is incomplete and does not comply with the Political Reform Act. Designated employees must use only the FPPC-prescribed Form 730, and those who previously filed using the city's incomplete form should file amended Initial Statements using the proper FPPC form.

embedding.summary: FPPC consultant Carla Wardlow confirms in writing to the City of Oakland that the city's locally-prepared Form 730 for Statements of Economic Interests is incomplete and non-compliant, and directs that designated employees must use the FPPC-prescribed form and file amended statements.
